title: CBP Agents Kill ICU Nurse Alex Pretti at Minneapolis Protest
excerpt: Border Patrol agents fatally shot Alex Pretti, a 37-year-old VA hospital ICU nurse, during protests against Operation Metro Surge in Minneapolis. Video evidence contradicted official accounts that he posed a threat, and federal agents physically blocked state investigators from the scene.
content:
On January 24, 2026, Alex Jeffrey Pretti, a 37-year-old intensive care nurse for the U.S. Department of Veterans Affairs, was shot multiple times and killed by two U.S. Customs and Border Protection officers near 26th Street and Nicollet Avenue in the Whittier neighborhood of Minneapolis.

The killing occurred amid widespread protests against Operation Metro Surge. Videos verified by Reuters, the BBC, The Wall Street Journal, and NBC News showed Pretti holding only a phone before being tackled. An agent removed Pretti's legally carried firearm from its holster before the first shot was fired. A total of 10 shots were fired; six were fired at Pretti's motionless body on the ground.

  • DHS labeled Pretti a 'domestic terrorist' and said officers fired fearing for their lives — a narrative contradicted by multiple bystander videos.
  • ProPublica identified the two shooters as Border Patrol agent Jesus Ochoa and CBP officer Raymundo Gutierrez, both assigned to Operation Metro Surge. CBP refused to release their names.
  • Federal agents physically blocked Minnesota Bureau of Criminal Apprehension investigators from the scene, prompting emergency litigation and a temporary restraining order against evidence destruction.
  • Minneapolis Police Chief O'Hara noted Pretti's death was the city's third federal-officer shooting in under three weeks, and that two of Minneapolis's three 2026 homicides had been committed by federal agents.
  • State officials specifically cited Pretti's case in demanding access to Agent Jonathan Ross's training and personnel records, underscoring the timeline's central theme of training accountability.
"He had a phone in one hand … and his other hand was raised above his head while trying to protect the woman ICE just pushed down." — statement from Pretti's parents

imageAlt: CBP Agents Kill ICU Nurse Alex Pretti at Minneapolis Protest
